name: codex
title: Codex
description: Run OpenAI Codex tasks, manage sessions, and launch agentic workflows
author: mileschu

commands.name: run-task
commands.title: Run Codex Task
commands.description: Run a prompt with OpenAI Codex

commands.name: sessions
commands.title: Codex Sessions
commands.description: View and resume recent Codex sessions

commands.name: saved-prompts
commands.title: Saved Prompts
commands.description: Manage and launch saved Codex prompts

tools.name: list-codex-projects
tools.title: List Codex Projects
tools.description: Find local project directories that Codex can use as a working directory. Use this when the user names a project but does not give an exact path.

root.description: Optional absolute path, ~/ path, or omitted to search ~/Projects.

query.description: Optional project-name filter, such as raycast or command-center.

limit.description: Maximum number of projects to return. Defaults to 40.

tools.name: list-codex-sessions
tools.title: List Codex Sessions
tools.description: List recent Codex sessions from ~/.codex/session_index.jsonl so a user can resume or reference prior Codex work.

query.description: Optional case-insensitive search over the session title.

limit.description: Maximum number of sessions to return. Defaults to 20.

tools.name: list-codex-workflows
tools.title: List Codex Workflows
tools.description: List built-in Codex workflows and saved prompts. Use this before running a named workflow or saved prompt.

query.description: Optional filter for workflow ID, title, or description.

includeSavedPrompts.description: Whether to include saved prompts from Raycast LocalStorage. Defaults to true.

limit.description: Maximum number of workflows to return. Defaults to 50.

tools.name: run-codex-workflow
tools.title: Run Codex Workflow
tools.description: Run the OpenAI Codex CLI in a local project directory using a built-in workflow, saved prompt, or custom prompt. This can inspect code and, when fullAuto is true, edit files.

prompt.description: The user request for Codex. Required unless savedPromptId points to a saved prompt.

directory.description: Working directory for Codex. Use an absolute path, ~/ path, or a project name under ~/Projects. If omitted, the extension default directory is used.

workflow.description: Built-in workflow ID. Valid values: custom, plan, implement, review, debug, test, docs, ship.

savedPromptId.description: Optional saved prompt ID returned by list-codex-workflows.

model.description: Optional Codex model override, such as gpt-5.4 or gpt-5.3-codex-spark.

fullAuto.description: Whether to allow Codex full-auto file writes. Defaults to the selected workflow's safety mode.

sessionId.description: Optional Codex session ID to resume.

timeoutSeconds.description: Maximum runtime before the Codex subprocess is stopped. Defaults to 180 and is capped at 900.

tools.name: save-codex-prompt
tools.title: Save Codex Prompt
tools.description: Save a reusable Codex prompt into this extension's saved prompts library.

title.description: Optional short title. If omitted, the first line of the prompt is used.

ai.instructions: Use this extension to run OpenAI Codex CLI workflows from Raycast. If the user asks about a project without an exact path, call list-codex-projects first. Use list-codex-workflows when the user asks for available workflows or saved prompts. Use list-codex-sessions when the user wants to resume, continue, or inspect prior Codex work. Use run-codex-workflow to execute Codex. Prefer workflow=plan or workflow=review with fullAuto=false for planning and reviews. Use workflow=implement, debug, test, or docs when the user explicitly asks Codex to make changes; the tool confirmation will show the side effects before execution. Always mention the working directory and sessionId in the response when a run starts or completes. Keep final answers concise and surface stderr or timeout details if Codex fails.

evals.input: @codex review the current diff in raycast-codex
@codex implement a small fix in raycast-codex and run validation

preferences.name: codexPath
preferences.title: Codex Binary Path
preferences.description: Full path to the codex binary

preferences.name: defaultDirectory
preferences.title: Default Working Directory
preferences.description: Default directory for @Codex workflow runs when no project path is provided
